title: AUC-safe regime blend with parametric calibration, not isotonic
summary: Calibration docs warn isotonic can create ties and change ROC AUC.
rationale: Calibration docs warn isotonic can create ties and change ROC AUC. For this competition, a better use of calibration is regime-aware blending: combine specialists for dry vs wet/intermediate, early vs late race, or first-stop vs later-stop regimes, then apply Platt or beta-style monotone calibration on out-of-fold predictions only if it preserves rank quality.
implementation_hint: Train 2-4 specialists on disjoint strategy regimes, convert each OOF prediction to rank, and blend ranks with a simple meta-learner or weighted average. If probability calibration is needed for submission stability, prefer logistic/beta-style post-processing over isotonic and verify no AUC drop on OOF.
expected_effect: Small but robust uplift from letting different models dominate different pit regimes while avoiding AUC loss from tied calibrated outputs.
risk: Too many specialists can overfit fold noise; calibration may be unnecessary because ROC AUC only depends on ranking.
